title: 2010 Pakistan Super Floods
description: The 2010 Pakistan floods were one of the worst in the country's history. Triggered by abnormally heavy monsoon rains, approximately one-fifth of Pakistan's total land area was underwater at the peak.

title: 2011 Sindh Floods
description: Heavy monsoon rains caused severe flooding in southern Pakistan, particularly Sindh. Millions were displaced and large areas of agricultural land were damaged.

title: 2014 Pakistan Floods
description: Heavy monsoon rains caused flooding in central Punjab and Azad Kashmir, with the Chenab and Ravi rivers overflowing their banks.

title: 2022 Pakistan Catastrophic Floods
description: The 2022 floods were unprecedented in scale. Extreme monsoon rainfall — linked to climate change — caused flooding across one-third of Pakistan. Sindh and Balochistan were worst affected. Damages exceeded USD 14.9 billion (World Bank PDNA).
